name: Basic Pizza Dough
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Warm tap water (110 to 115 degrees)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the warm water into a medium-sized mixing bowl and sprinkle in the yeast. Stir gently with a fork until the yeast has dissolved and the liquid turns light beige in color.
2. Add 1 cup of the flour and salt. Mix thoroughly with a wooden spoon. Add a second cup of flour to the bowl and mix well. After the second cup of flour is mixed in, the dough should start coming away from the sides of the bowl and should begin to form a soft, sticky mass.
3. Measure out the third cup of flour. Sprinkle some on the work surface and flour your hands generously. Remove all of the dough from the bowl and begin working it by kneading additional flour in gradually.
4. To knead the dough, use the heel of your hands to push the dough across the floured work surface in one sweep. Clench the dough in your fist and twist and fold it over. Use a dough scraper to help gather any wet dough that sticks to the work surface into a ball while kneading. Repeat this process, adding only as much flour as needed to prevent sticking. Work quickly and avoid being delicate. Squeeze and push the dough around to develop its gluten and facilitate rolling out.
5. When the dough no longer feels sticky, press the heel of your hand down into it and hold for 10 seconds. If your hand comes up clean, the dough is ready. If it sticks, a bit more kneading will be necessary. Continue kneading until the dough is smooth, elastic (it should spring back when pressed), and free of visible flour lines. The process should take about 5 to 10 minutes.
6. Lightly oil a 2 quart bowl with vegetable oil. Roll the ball of dough around in the bowl to coat it with a thin film of oil. Tightly seal the bowl with plastic wrap to trap moisture and heat from the yeast's carbon dioxide gases, helping the dough rise faster.
7. Place the bowl in a warm, draft-free place. Let the dough rise for 30 to 45 minutes.
8. Once the dough has doubled in bulk, punch it down by pressing your fist into it. The gases will quickly escape, and the dough will collapse. Remove the dough from the bowl and knead briefly (about 1 minute).
9. The dough is now ready to be patted and rolled into pizza or undergo further rising.
10. To allow for a second rise, add a bit more oil to the bowl and repeat the procedure described in step 6. Then the dough is ready for shaping.
